title: Intensely Smoky Malaysian Beef Quesadilla
description: A halal-friendly Malaysian dish featuring tortillas, smoke, and fermented ingredients, perfect for curing hangovers, this smoky beef quesadilla combines the flavors of Malaysia with the comfort of a Mexican dish.
instructions: Preheat a large skillet over medium-high heat with 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil.
Add the diced onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the smoked beef and cook until it is heated through, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the malaysian chili paste, fermented shrimp paste, and soy sauce, stirring to combine.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the beef mixture for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
In a large pan or griddle, warm the tortillas over medium heat for 30 seconds on each side.
Spoon about 1/4 cup of the beef mixture onto half of each tortilla, then fold the tortilla in half to enclose.
Cook the quesadillas for 2-3 minutes on each side, until they are crispy and the filling is heated through.
Repeat with the remaining tortillas and filling.
